title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. suaveolens
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. suaveolens, also known as Wild Angelica or Garden Angelica, is a perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as coughs, colds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Wild Angelica include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Wild Angelica often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or externally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
